compiled_state.title: record-test-status.sh: directly-staged RED test file -> failures not tolerated despite .test-index marker
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Expected Behavior
When a test file with a RED marker in .test-index is staged directly (not via its mapped source file), record-test-status.sh should still look up the RED marker from .test-index and tolerate failures at or after the marker boundary.

## Actual Behavior
record-test-status.sh line 502-515: when a staged file passes fuzzy_is_test_file(), it is added to ASSOCIATED_TESTS with an empty marker (_TEST_MARKER_MAP[file]='') and the loop continues — skipping the .test-index lookup entirely. The RED marker is only consulted when the SOURCE file (e.g., SKILL.md) is staged and the .test-index entry is processed via the source→test mapping path (line 540+). This means staging a RED test file directly always records it as 'failed' regardless of .test-index markers.

## Reproduction
1. Create a test file with RED failures: tests/scripts/test-sprint-primary-ticket-model.sh
2. Add .test-index entry: plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md:tests/scripts/test-sprint-primary-ticket-model.sh [test_primary_ticket_in_phase1]
3. Stage only the test file (not SKILL.md): git add tests/scripts/test-sprint-primary-ticket-model.sh
4. Run: bash plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh
5. Observe: status=failed, no RED zone tolerance applied

## Root Cause
plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh line 502: fuzzy_is_test_file check short-circuits before .test-index lookup. The marker map is set to empty string (no marker) and 'continue' skips the index path.

## Suggested Fix
After the fuzzy_is_test_file path (line 502-515), also check .test-index for entries where the test file appears on the RIGHT side. If found with a [marker], populate _TEST_MARKER_MAP with that marker instead of empty.
